BOOK NOW

Underwater Treadmill (Hydrotherapy)

Low-impact aquatic therapy that builds strength, improves mobility, and accelerates recovery while reducing stress on joints and supporting cardiovascular health.

BOOK NOW

Common Conditions That Respond Well

Hip & Elbow Dysplasia
TPLO / Cruciate Repair Recovery
Intervertebral Disc Disease (IVDD)
Degenerative Myelopathy

Fibrocartilaginous Embolism (FCE)

Osteoarthritis & Spondylosis

Obesity & Weight Management

Post-Fracture Rehabilitation

Muscle Atrophy & Weakness

Stroke / Neurological Recovery

Pre-Surgical Conditioning

Chronic Lameness & Gait Issues

See Hydrotherapy in Action

WHAT TO EXPECT

Your Pet's First Session

Calm, gentle, and always at your pet's pace. Most animals warm up to the water within minutes.

1

Holistic Assessment

Your integrative vet reviews history, conducts a gait and mobility evaluation, and designs a personalized aquatic protocol.

2

Tank Introduction

Your pet is gently guided into the tank as water rises to the therapeutic level. A therapist is always hands-on throughout.

3

Active Session

Belt speed and water level are adjusted for your pet's specific needs. Sessions last 30–40 minutes.

PET PARENT STORIES

Paws on the Treadmill, Tails in the Air

"After Biscuit's TPLO surgery, the surgeon said he'd be limping for months. After six underwater treadmill sessions at Healing Paws, he was walking normally. We were blown away."

— Maria T., Labrador mom

Fort Lauderdale

"Our 14-year-old golden couldn't climb stairs anymore. Three weeks of aquatic therapy and she's back on the bed every night. I genuinely didn't think that was possible."

— James R., senior dog dad

Boca Raton

Your Questions, Answered

Pet Hydrotherapy Appointments Available!

Schedule a consultation to explore how underwater treadmill therapy can help your pet move freely and pain-free.
